spec_match.rationale: The playbook follows ChromeStatus: Chrome 138 is the final update stream for macOS 11, Chrome 139+ requires macOS 12+ for new installs, and macOS 11 visitors need upgrade guidance.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised audience, impact, milestone, banner date, detection method, visitor state, placement, cadence, and tone controls; each changed the live playbook, generated copy, or detection snippet.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page delivers the feature-card promise of a tailored communication and technical playbook for unsupported macOS 11 visitors.

edge_cases.rationale: DevTools verification covered existing macOS 11, blocked new-install macOS 11, supported macOS 12+, and unknown/hints-unavailable branches; supported and unknown branches no longer show the fallback-build guidance.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps local styling on shared CSS variables, has no raw hex scan hits, and uses interactive generated output rather than a static card.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 at /v139/chrome-removes-support-for-macos-11/replacement-playbook/?verify=after-replacement-playbook. The default UA-CH/macOS 11 existing-install branch rendered the playbook and copy; Enterprise, High impact, Chrome 145, banner date 2026-09-15, client-side JS detection, macOS 11 new install, account placement, every-page cadence, and urgent tone all updated the visible output, including modal escalation and JS detection snippet. The browser pass found and repaired a contradiction where high-impact supported or unknown visitors still received fallback-build guidance; after the patch, confirmed macOS 11 still shows the fallback build, supported macOS 12+ suppresses the banner and clears stale audience membership, and unknown platform keeps neutral copy with compatibility_platform_unknown measurement. Concept route network requests were 200/204 with no console messages. The feature conformance route /v139/chrome-removes-support-for-macos-11/conformance/?verify=replacement-playbook-patched reported 5 pass / 0 fail with no console messages.
